element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial-like introduction to weapon switching mechanics
Weapon effectiveness varies by monster type; silverware kills werewolves in one hit, crucifixes damage vampires faster
Tourist neighbors transform into werewolves at nightfall and become unsaveable, adding time pressure
Requires understanding enemy-weapon matchups; each weapon has distinct effectiveness
Temporary powerful state grants tactical advantage; temporary monster form turns player into strong unit
7 optional bonus levels provide risk-reward scoring opportunities
Climactic confrontation with game's antagonist after defeating subordinate monsters

typical_relief_triggers: Rescuing all neighbors on a level
Door opening to next stage after completion
Clearing enemy clusters with area-effect weapons
typical_tension_triggers: Neighbors in immediate danger from approaching monsters
Daylight-to-night transition turning tourists into unsaveable werewolves
Encountering unfamiliar monster types requiring weapon adaptation
Monster density increases in later levels
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based tension with escalating monster variety and density; environmental time-pressure mechanics introduce cognitive tension alongside combat tension

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Keys unlock doors to optional paths; bonus levels accessible via branching routes

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to basic weapons and suburban monster types with straightforward rescue objectives
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Monster variety increases; environmental hazards like day-night cycles introduce time pressure; thematic zones (shopping malls, pyramids, castles) present varied spatial layouts
difficulty_curve.late_game: Dense monster encounters; complex enemy interactions; final confrontation with Dr. Tongue; higher neighbor count requiring efficient routing
notable_spikes: Transition to supernatural/exotic zones (pyramids, castles) with increased density
Final boss encounter with Dr. Tongue

notable_level_patterns.description: Game progresses through distinct thematic environments: suburban neighborhoods → shopping malls → pyramids → haunted castles. Each theme introduces monster types matching that environment (tourists in malls, mummies in pyramids, chainsaw maniacs in castles)

Level completion requires rescuing at least one neighbor; all neighbors can be lost if touched by enemies. Creates risk management: player must balance offensive play with defensive neighbor protection
Some levels feature day-to-night cycle where tourists transform into werewolves, becoming unsaveable. Creates implicit time limit and strategic decisions about level clearing speed
Specific weapons have amplified effectiveness against themed enemies (silverware vs werewolves, crucifixes vs vampires). Rewards player learning and adaptation to monster types
